steps.title: Read the phases of the run from the graph
steps.content:
The speed-vs-time graph splits Theresa's 30-minute run into segments:
  • 0–5 min: speed increases from 0 to 7 mph
  • 5–10 min: speed stays constant at 7 mph
  • 10–20 min: speed decreases from 7 to 5 mph
  • 20–25 min: speed increases from 5 to 8 mph (peak)
  • 25–30 min: speed decreases from 8 to 0 mph

steps.title: Total time increasing vs. decreasing
steps.content: Increasing: 5 + 5 = 10 minutes. Decreasing: 10 + 5 = 15 minutes. So the speed was decreasing for a longer period than it was increasing, not the other way around.

steps.title: Check each statement
steps.content:
• A) Constant speed for 5 minutes (from 5–10 min) — TRUE, eliminate.
  • B) Increasing for longer than decreasing — FALSE (10 min increasing vs. 15 min decreasing).
  • C) Speed decreased at a constant rate the last 5 minutes (25–30, a straight line from 8 to 0) — TRUE, eliminate.
  • D) Max speed (8 mph) reached during the last 10 minutes (at 20–25 min) — TRUE, eliminate.
The only statement that is NOT true is B. That matches choice B — B is correct.
